topic: Leave-one-cluster sign-flip phase for two-way fixed-effect event-study ATT estimates. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille negative-weight decompositions, Sun-Abraham event-study contamination, and cluster jackknife influence diagnostics; closest banked proposals are q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov and eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier_v1.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the exact finite-sample region in which a full-sample TWFE event-study ATT contrast is positive but recomputing the same residualized contrast after deleting one cluster makes it negative.

source_refs.claim: TWFE DiD with staggered timing is a weighted average of all possible two-group/two-period comparisons, clarifying the full-sample identifying variation.
source_refs.author: Goodman-Bacon

source_refs.claim: TWFE estimators estimate weighted sums of group-period effects and the weights may be negative, so signs can reverse even when effects are positive.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.claim: Event-study lead and lag coefficients under staggered adoption can be contaminated by treatment effects from other relative periods.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: Cluster leverage, partial leverage, influence, and jackknife diagnostics identify clustered regression designs where inference is challenging.
source_refs.author: MacKinnon, Nielsen, and Webb

source_refs.excerpt: withdraws any claim that the sign of FWL weights is determined by (rho_T)
without an additional stationary-share balance parameter

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published TWFE/event-study papers identify full-sample weighting and contamination mechanisms, while the cluster-diagnostics paper identifies leverage and influence for clustered regressions; neither side gives a recomputed leave-one-cluster phase boundary for an event-study ATT contrast after the fixed effects and residualized event-study regressor are refit. The mined q1 proposal also flags that a one-parameter spectral sign rule is insufficient because stationary-share balance enters the FWL geometry.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to read the sign flip from existing negative-weight decompositions fails because Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille decompose the full-sample TWFE estimand, while MacKinnon-Nielsen-Webb-style leverage diagnostics change when the cluster is deleted and the event-study residuals are recomputed.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An exact finite-dimensional sign-flip criterion, expressed as a table of cluster residualized leverage h_g and signed cohort-time score s_g with a sharp threshold for when the leave-one-cluster contrast crosses zero.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive primitive cluster/cohort loading matrices for TWFE, BJS, Sun-Abraham, Callaway-Sant'Anna, and Wooldridge event-study ATT estimators and determine which one-cluster deletions change only influence projection versus the estimand itself.

source_refs.claim: BJS recast event-study estimation as imputation plus weighting and derive robust finite-sample efficient estimation under their framework.
source_refs.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess

source_refs.claim: Callaway-Sant'Anna identify and aggregate group-time ATT parameters in multiple-period DiD with variation in treatment timing.
source_refs.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Sun-Abraham give interaction-weighted event-study estimation to avoid contamination of dynamic TWFE coefficients.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: Wooldridge develops extended TWFE/pooling formulations for cohort-time ATT estimation and equivalences in structured designs.
source_refs.author: Wooldridge

source_refs.excerpt: Define E_m and Z_m entrywise for BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge
does not derive those matrices from explicit estimator equations

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature gives estimator-specific targets, weights, and efficiency claims, but the prior proposal failed because its contrast matrices were stipulated rather than derived from the named estimating equations. For the present topic, deletion of a cluster can alter comparison support and residualized loadings, so a source-derived loading table is the missing prerequisite for distinguishing a pure influence sign flip from an estimand change.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to stack published estimator weights fails because the eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier reviewer explicitly reports that E_m and Z_m must be defined entrywise and that the earlier matrices were not derived from estimator equations.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite estimator-loading construction that maps cohort shares, event-time support, and cluster deletion indicators into explicit L_TWFE, L_BJS, L_SA, L_CS, and L_W rows, with a necessary-and-sufficient condition for equality of the target and inequality of the leave-one-cluster influence projection.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a minimal staggered-adoption panel in which the full-sample event-study ATT target is unchanged across estimators but a single high-leverage cluster deletion flips the TWFE contrast sign while modern ATT estimators do not flip.

source_refs.claim: Negative TWFE weights can make the regression coefficient negative even when all underlying effects are positive.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.claim: The timing-variation decomposition accounts for how different two-by-two comparisons enter the full TWFE coefficient.
source_refs.author: Goodman-Bacon

source_refs.claim: The modern DiD synthesis distinguishes problematic TWFE practice from heterogeneity-robust staggered-adoption estimators.
source_refs.author: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe

source_refs.excerpt: TWFE decomposition/negative weights, not equal-target unequal-IF projections
explicit 3-cohort x 4-period witness where the four estimators identify the same ATT

open_problems.why_unsolved: Negative-weight and timing-decomposition papers explain why full-sample TWFE can have unintuitive signs, and modern DiD papers explain alternative ATT targets; the mined reviewer note says the missing object is an equal-target unequal-influence witness rather than another decomposition. The unresolved gap is a finite witness separating TWFE-specific deletion fragility from generic heterogeneity-robust estimator movement.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to use a negative-weight example fails because the prior reviewer states that Goodman-Bacon and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille cover TWFE decomposition/negative weights, not equal-target unequal-IF projections.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A hand-checkable 4-cluster by 4-period adoption and outcome table with all estimator targets aligned, together with computed full-sample and leave-one-cluster contrasts showing TWFE sign reversal and nonreversal for at least one modern ATT estimator.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop valid inference for the post-diagnostic statistic that at least one leave-one-cluster recomputation reverses the sign of a TWFE event-study ATT estimate.

source_refs.claim: DiD inference is nonstandard when the number of policy-changing groups is small.
source_refs.author: Conley and Taber

source_refs.claim: Cluster-robust and wild-bootstrap inference can fail when there are few treated clusters.
source_refs.author: MacKinnon and Webb

source_refs.claim: Small-sample cluster-robust adjustments are needed for fixed-effect models with clustered errors.
source_refs.author: Pustejovsky and Tipton
source_refs.venue: Journal of Business and Economic Statistics

source_refs.claim: Regression uncertainty depends on whether the target is sampling-based or design-based, which affects clustering interpretation.
source_refs.author: Abadie, Athey, Imbens, and Wooldridge

source_refs.claim: Recent CSDID work reports few-cluster inference problems and proposes cluster-jackknife standard errors.
source_refs.author: Karim, Nielsen, MacKinnon, and Webb

open_problems.why_unsolved: The inference literature treats few treated clusters, cluster-robust variance corrections, wild bootstrap, and jackknife standard errors, but the web scan found no method that conditions on selecting the sign-flip event after inspecting all leave-one-cluster paths. That selection event is not a standard ATT estimator, standard t statistic, or ordinary jackknife variance estimate.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to report a cluster-jackknife standard error fails because Conley-Taber and MacKinnon-Webb show few-cluster DiD inference can be distorted, while the selected event of some deletion crossing zero is a max-over-deletions diagnostic rather than the mean estimator those procedures target.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A selective-inference or finite-sample bound theorem for the sign-flip indicator or minimum leave-one-cluster signed margin, with explicit conditions under which its rejection probability is controlled.

open_problems.open_problem: Decompose sign reversals of event-study lead coefficients into cluster-leverage effects, Sun-Abraham contamination from heterogeneous treatment effects, and pretrend-test selection effects.

source_refs.claim: Apparent pretrends in event-study regressions can arise solely from treatment-effect heterogeneity under staggered adoption.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: Pretesting for parallel trends has important limitations and can change event-study interpretation.
source_refs.author: Roth

source_refs.claim: Pre-event trends in panel event-study designs require careful modeling, with proxy-based approaches for latent trends.
source_refs.author: Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, and Shapiro

source_refs.claim: BJS emphasize separating identification-assumption testing from estimation in event-study designs.
source_refs.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess

open_problems.why_unsolved: Lead coefficients in staggered event studies are already ambiguous because they may reflect heterogeneous post-treatment effects or pretrend-test conditioning; adding leave-one-cluster recomputation introduces a separate cluster-leverage channel. The scan found no statement-level result separating these three causes in one finite event-study design.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to interpret a deleted-cluster lead sign flip as evidence of a pretrend fails because Sun-Abraham show apparent pretrends can be generated by treatment-effect heterogeneity and Roth shows pretesting itself changes the evidential meaning of event-study estimates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite decomposition formula for a lead-coefficient leave-one-cluster sign change into a residualized leverage term, a contamination term from nonlead treatment effects, and a pretest-selection term or counterexample showing the decomposition is impossible without extra restrictions.

literature_map:
1. Goodman-Bacon (2021, JoE) decomposes staggered TWFE DiD into full-sample two-by-two comparisons; this anchors the timing-weight side of the topic.
2. de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) show TWFE weights on group-period effects may be negative and can reverse signs; this is the closest negative-weight comparator.
3. Sun and Abraham (2021, JoE) show event-study leads/lags are contaminated by other relative-period effects; this anchors dynamic TWFE event-study contamination.
4. Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, ReStud) recast event studies as imputation plus weighting and give a robust efficient comparator, but not a leave-one-cluster sign law.
5. Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, JoE), Sant'Anna and Zhao (2020, JoE), Gardner (2022), and Wooldridge (2023, Econometrics Journal) provide modern staggered-DiD comparators for separating TWFE-specific fragility from generic estimator movement.
6. Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe (2023, JoE) synthesize the modern DiD toolkit and support positioning the target as a diagnostic, not another estimator.
7. Roth (2022, AER Insights) and Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, and Shapiro (2019, AER) make pretrend interpretation delicate, especially for lead sign flips.
8. Kline, Saggio, and Solvsten (2020, Econometrica) supply leave-out linear-model machinery, but for variance components rather than TWFE event-study sign boundaries.
9. MacKinnon, Nielsen, and Webb (2023, Stata Journal) define cluster leverage, influence, and jackknife diagnostics; they do not derive TWFE event-study ATT sign thresholds.
10. Conley and Taber (2011, ReStat), MacKinnon and Webb (2018, Econometrics Journal), Pustejovsky and Tipton (2018, JBES), and Abadie et al. (2020, Econometrica) frame the inference risks around few/high-leverage clusters.
11. Karim, Nielsen, MacKinnon, and Webb (2026, arXiv) extends cluster-jackknife concerns to CSDID, making the TWFE-vs-modern-estimator comparison live as of 2026.
12. The open gap is therefore a finite leave-one-cluster sign-flip phase law for residualized TWFE/event-study contrasts, not another full-sample negative-weight decomposition or routine jackknife variance formula.
prior_proposal_map:
No substantive prior proposal exists yet for panel_jackknife_signflip_phase_v1; the active research JSONL only records init.
Closest named banked ancestor is q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov: it has event-window/FWL rank material and explicitly withdraws scalar rho-based sign claims.
Closest estimator-frontier ancestor is eid_bjs_if_contrast_frontier_v1, which targets BJS/SA/CS/Wooldridge event-study ATT IF contrasts but failed for promissory C/Omega/Pi objects.
eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 is the immediate predecessor: useful active-preperiod rank and Z_M/P_M ideas, but K_star/Gamma remained vague.
The most usable Track-A pivot is to derive primitive estimator/cluster loadings first, then ask for a leave-one-cluster sign-flip threshold.
Secondary adjacent templates were Honest-DID zero-crossing active-face phases, bounded-anticipation signed comparison weights, and DRDID/IPWDID level-plus-tangent frontiers.
Study-pipeline LATE/Manski partial-ID gaps were inspected as augmentation candidates but dropped by topic relevance before G1-G6 filtering.
